title: The Fifth Sunday of Easter
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: April 28, 2024

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Fifth Sunday of Easter in the Christian calendar, falling within the Easter season (which runs from Easter Sunday through Pentecost). Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would likely include Acts 11:1-18 (Peter's defense of eating with Gentiles), Psalm 148, Revelation 21:1-6 (the new creation), and John 13:31-35 (Jesus's farewell discourse on love as the new commandment). This Sunday emphasizes the continuing resurrection life of the church and the inclusive nature of God's grace.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the lead-up to its 80th General Convention, scheduled for June 2024 in Louisville, Kentucky, with significant pre-convention discussions underway regarding sexuality, marriage, and ordination policies. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Glenda Wratten (who has served since 2018), was engaged in ongoing canonical and pastoral matters typical of spring in the church calendar. No major episcopal elections or extraordinary diocesan crises were reported in Colorado at this specific time.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver experienced typical late April spring weather with temperatures in the 60s-70s Fahrenheit and occasional afternoon thunderstorms common to the season. Mayor Mike Johnston's administration was actively implementing policies on homelessness and housing affordability, issues that had been headline news throughout early 2024. The Capitol Hill neighborhood, where Saint John's Cathedral is located, was experiencing ongoing revitalization and gentrification discussions, with the cathedral serving as a historic anchor institution in the area.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

President Joe Biden was managing ongoing concerns about his age and fitness for office, with Democratic insiders beginning private discussions about potential alternatives (public pressure would intensify significantly after the June debate). The Israel-Gaza conflict, following the October 2023 attacks, continued to dominate headlines with ongoing humanitarian concerns and international criticism. Former President Donald Trump was in the midst of multiple criminal trials, with the New York hush money trial underway and drawing daily media attention.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Russia-Ukraine conflict continued into its third year with no resolution in sight, with spring 2024 seeing renewed fighting intensity and Western weapons shipments remaining a contentious political issue. The situation in Haiti was deteriorating dramatically, with gang violence forcing Prime Minister Garry Conille to flee the country on April 25, 2024—just days before this sermon. Pakistan was dealing with severe heat waves and economic instability following earlier political turmoil and elections.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in late April 2024 was marked by deep political anxiety and polarization, with the 2024 election campaign intensifying despite eight months remaining until November. The Denver Nuggets basketball team had recently finished their regular season (ending April 14), with attention turning toward playoff hopes and the defending NBA champion's performance. There was a pervasive sense of institutional uncertainty—in politics, the church, media credibility—characteristic of mid-year 2024 in American life.
